title: arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. marschlinsii
intro: Arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. marschlinsii, also known as mountain sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, Caryophyllaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. marschlinsii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and act as a diuretic. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
